segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: At last, firemen have put out a big forest fire in California.

translations.zh-Hans: 消防队员终于扑灭了加利福尼亚的一场森林大火。

segments.text: Since then, they have been trying to find out how the fire began.

translations.zh-Hans: 从那以后，他们一直在设法查明起火原因。

segments.text: Forest fires are often caused by broken glass or by cigarette ends which people carelessly throw away.

translations.zh-Hans: 森林火灾常由碎玻璃或人们随手丢弃的烟头引起。

segments.text: Yesterday, the firemen examined the ground carefully, but were not able to find any broken glass.

translations.zh-Hans: 昨天，消防队员仔细检查了地面，却没能找到任何碎玻璃。

segments.text: They were also quite sure that a cigarette end did not start the fire.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们也十分确信，火灾不是由烟头引起的。

segments.text: This morning, however, a fireman accidentally discovered the cause.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而今天早晨，一名消防队员偶然发现了起火原因。

segments.text: He noticed the remains of a snake which was wound round the electric wires of a 16,000 volt power line.

translations.zh-Hans: 他注意到一条蛇的残骸缠绕在一条16,000伏高压线的电线上。

segments.text: In this way, he was able to solve the mystery.

translations.zh-Hans: 这样，他便解开了这个谜。

segments.text: The explanation was simple but very unusual.

translations.zh-Hans: 解释很简单，却十分不同寻常。

segments.text: A bird had snatched up the snake from the ground and then dropped it onto the wires.

translations.zh-Hans: 一只鸟从地面抓起那条蛇，随后把它丢到了电线上。

segments.text: The snake then wound itself round the wires.

translations.zh-Hans: 蛇接着把身体缠在电线上。

segments.text: When it did so, it sent sparks down to the ground and these immediately started a fire.

translations.zh-Hans: 它这样做时，火花落到地面，立刻引发了火灾。
